gcmotion.Plot#

class gcmotion.Plot(cwp)#

Component of class Particle. Contains all the plotting-related methods.

__init__(cwp)#

Copies attributes from cwp to self.

The instance itself is automatically initialized internally by the Particle class, and only its methods are to be called by the user, as cwp.plot.<method()>.

Parameters:

cwp (Particle) – The Current Working Particle

Methods

__init__(cwp)

Copies attributes from cwp to self.

contour_energy([theta_lim, psi_lim, ...])

Draws a 2D contour plot of the Hamiltonian.

drift([angle, lim])

Draws \(\theta - P_\theta\) plot.

drifts([theta_lim])

Draws 2 plots: 1] \(\theta-P_\theta\) and 2] \(\zeta-P_\zeta\).

fft()

Plots the timeseries and its FFT results.

orbit_point([different_colors, labels])

Plots the particle point on the \(\mu-P_\zeta\) (normalized) plane.

parabolas()

Constructs and plots the orbit type parabolas.

time_evolution([percentage])

Plots the time evolution of all the dynamical variables and canonical momenta.

tokamak_profile([zoom])

Plots the electric field, potential, and q factor, with respect to \(\psi/\psi_{wall}\).

torus2d([percentage, truescale])

Plots the poloidal and toroidal view of the orbit.

torus3d([percentage, truescale, hd, bold, ...])

Creates a 3d transparent torus and the particle's orbit.
tokamak_profile(zoom: list = [0, 1.1])#

Plots the electric field, potential, and q factor, with respect to \(\psi/\psi_{wall}\).

Parameters:

zoom (list, optional) – zoom to specific area in the x-axis of the electric field and potential plots. Defaults to [0, 1.1].

time_evolution(percentage: int = 100)#

Plots the time evolution of all the dynamical variables and canonical momenta.

Parameters:

percentage (int, optional) – The percentage of the orbit to be plotted. Defaults to 100.

drifts(theta_lim: list = [-3.141592653589793, 3.141592653589793])#

Draws 2 plots: 1] \(\theta-P_\theta\) and 2] \(\zeta-P_\zeta\).

Parameters:

theta_lim (list, optional) – Plot xlim. Must be either [0,2π] or [-π,π]. Defaults to [-π,π].

drift(angle: Literal['zeta', 'theta'] = 'theta', lim: list = [-3.141592653589793, 3.141592653589793], **kwargs)#

Draws \(\theta - P_\theta\) plot.

This method is called internally by countour_energy() as well.

Parameters:

  • theta_lim (list, optional) – Plot xlim. Must be either [0,2π] or [-π,π]. Defaults to [-π,π].

  • ax (pyplot ax, optional) – The pyplot ax object to plot upon.

_calcW_grid(theta: array, psi: array, Pz: float, contour_Phi: bool, units: str)#

Returns a single value or a grid of the calculated Hamiltonian.

Only to be called internally, by contour_energy()..

Parameters:

  • theta (np.array) – The \(\theta\) values.

  • psi (np.array) – The \(\psi\) values.

  • Pz (float) – The \(P_\zeta\) value.

  • contour_Phi (bool) – Whether or not to add the electric potential term \(e\Phi\).

  • units (str) – The energy units.

contour_energy(theta_lim: list = [-3.141592653589793, 3.141592653589793], psi_lim: str | list = 'auto', plot_drift: bool = True, contour_Phi: bool = True, units: Literal['normal', 'eV', 'keV'] = 'keV', levels: int = None, wall_shade: bool = True, **kwargs)#

Draws a 2D contour plot of the Hamiltonian.

Can also plot the current particle’s \(\theta-P_\theta\) drift. Should be False when running with multiple initial conditions.

Parameters:

  • theta_lim (list, optional) – Plot xlim. Must be either [0,2π] or [-π,π]. Defaults to [-π,π].

  • psi_lim (list | str, optional) – If a list is passed, it plots between the 2 values relative to \(\psi_{wall}\). Defaults to ‘auto’.

  • plot_drift (bool, optional) – Whether or not to plot \(\theta-P_\theta\) drift on top. Defaults to True.

  • contour_Phi (bool, optional) – Whether or not to add the Φ term in the energy contour. Defaults to True.

  • units (str, optional) – The energy units. Must be ‘normal’, ‘eV’ or ‘keV’. Defaults to keV. Defaults to “keV”.

  • levels (int, optional) – The number of contour levels. Defaults to Config setting.

  • wall_shade (bool, optional) – Whether to shade the region \(\psi/\psi_{wall} > 1\). Defaults to True.

parabolas()#

Constructs and plots the orbit type parabolas.

Returns early if there is no Electric field.

orbit_point(different_colors=False, labels=True)#

Plots the particle point on the \(\mu-P_\zeta\) (normalized) plane.

_toruspoints(percentage: int = 100, truescale: bool = True) → tuple#

Calculates the toroidal coordionates of the particles orbit, \((r, \theta, \zeta)\).

\(r = \sqrt{2\psi}\) rather than \(\psi\) itself is used for the plot, since it is a better representation of the actual orbit.

Parameters:

  • percentage (int, optional) – The percentage of the orbit to be plotted. Defaults to 100.

  • truescale (bool, optional) – Whether or not to use the actual tokamak dimensions, or fit them around the orbit for better visibility. Defaults to True.

Returns:

The major and minor radii of the (possibly scaled) tokamak and the toroidal coordionates of the particles orbit. \((r, \theta, \zeta)\).

Return type:

5-tuple of np.arrays

torus2d(percentage: int = 100, truescale: bool = False)#

Plots the poloidal and toroidal view of the orbit.

Parameters:

  • percentage (int, optional) – 0-100: the percentage of the orbit to be plotted. Defaults to 100.

  • truescale (bool, optional) – Whether or not to construct the torus and orbit with the actual units of R and r. Defaults to True.

torus3d(percentage: int = 100, truescale: bool = False, hd: bool = False, bold: str = 'default', white_background: bool = True)#

Creates a 3d transparent torus and the particle’s orbit.

Parameters:

  • percentage (int, optional) – 0-100: the percentage of the orbit to be plotted. Defaults to 100.

  • truescale (bool, optional) – Whether or not to construct the torus and orbit with the actual units of R and r. Defaults to True.

  • hd (bool, optional) – High definition image (dpi = 900). Defaults to False (dpi = 300).

  • bold (str, optional) – The “boldness” level. Levels are “bold”, “BOLD”, or any. Defaults to Config settings.

  • white_background (bool, optional) – Whether to paint the background white or not. Overwrites the default plt.style(). Defaults to True.

fft()#

Plots the timeseries and its FFT results.

Note

Even though the time evolution plot only shows a few periods for clarity, the FFT is calculated across the full orbit.

gcmotion.Collection
